summary: Degree symbol (°) renders as incorrect glyph when drawn alongside Chinese characters using PingFang SC font on iOS 18, due to Apple changing the PingFang font format in iOS 18.

bugSignals.errorMessage: Degree symbol (°) renders as incorrect/garbled glyph alongside Chinese text on iOS 18

stepsToReproduce: Create a MAUI or iOS app using SkiaSharp
Use SKTypeface.FromFamilyName to load PingFang SC (or let iOS auto-select for Chinese text)
Draw text containing both Chinese characters and the degree symbol (°) using SKCanvas.DrawText
Run on iOS 18.0+ device or simulator
Observe the degree symbol renders as incorrect glyph (rectangle with circle inside)

screenshots.description: iOS 17 — correct rendering of degree symbol
iOS 18 — incorrect rendering of degree symbol
iOS 18 — degree char alone rendered as rectangle with circle

repoLinks.description: Minimal repro project with SkiaSharp, CoreGraphics, and CoreText comparison

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: Issue persists across both 2.88.x and 3.118.x — the root cause is in Skia's CoreText font handling layer which hasn't been updated for iOS 18 PingFang font format changes.

regression.reason: Reporter confirms the degree symbol rendered correctly on iOS 17.5 with same code and SkiaSharp version. The regression is caused by Apple changing the PingFang SC font format in iOS 18, not by a SkiaSharp code change.

analysis.summary: The degree symbol (°, U+00B0) renders as an incorrect glyph when drawn with PingFang SC on iOS 18. Apple changed the PingFang font to a new undocumented format in iOS 18 (confirmed via Apple Developer Forums). Skia reads font tables via CTFontCopyTable/CGFontCopyTableForTag — if the new font format affects how glyph mappings (cmap table) are exposed through CoreText, Skia would map the degree symbol to a wrong glyph ID. System text renderers (UILabel, CoreText) handle this via higher-level font fallback, but Skia's low-level DrawText uses a single typeface without automatic fallback.
analysis.rationale: Classified as type/bug because previously working rendering broke on iOS 18. Area is area/libSkiaSharp.native because the root cause is in Skia's CoreText font port (SkTypeface_mac_ct.cpp, SkFontMgr_mac_ct.cpp) which reads font data from the OS — this is upstream of the C API and C# layers. Severity is medium: wrong output with a workaround available (use HarfBuzz shaper or explicit font fallback).

keySignals.text: I've experiencing this regression on iOS 18 when drawing the degree symbol °
keySignals.interpretation: Clear regression tied to iOS version, not SkiaSharp version.

keySignals.text: It works on all iOS versions prior to 18. It also works in Japanese and Korean on iOS 18.
keySignals.interpretation: Language-specific — PingFang SC (Chinese) is affected, but JP/KR fonts are not.

keySignals.text: PingFang.ttc is no longer in /System/Library/Fonts/Core. iOS 18 has a new Chinese UI font.
keySignals.interpretation: Apple confirmed the PingFang font was moved and changed to a new format in iOS 18.

keySignals.text: Just tested on 3.118.0-preview.1.2 and the issue is still there.
keySignals.interpretation: Not fixed by SkiaSharp version upgrades — confirms this is an upstream Skia/CoreText interaction issue.

keySignals.text: what is to investigate here is how the PingFang SC font changed
keySignals.interpretation: Community member correctly identifies root cause as font-level change, not SkiaSharp code bug.

codeInvestigation.finding: Skia reads font tables via CTFontCopyTable and CGFontCopyTableForTag. If iOS 18's new PingFang format changes how these CoreText APIs expose glyph mappings, Skia would read incorrect cmap data, mapping the degree symbol to a wrong glyph.

onMatchFamilyStyleCharacter uses CTFontCreateForString for font fallback. However, this is only called by SKFontManager.MatchCharacter — basic DrawText with a specific typeface does NOT use font fallback, so if PingFang SC's degree symbol glyph mapping changed, DrawText will render the wrong glyph.
DrawText creates an SKTextBlob from text+font, then draws it. No font fallback logic — uses exactly the typeface provided. If the font maps U+00B0 to a wrong glyph, DrawText renders it as-is.

workarounds: Use SkiaSharp.HarfBuzz SKShaper for text shaping which may handle font fallback better
Use a bundled font file instead of the system PingFang SC font (SKTypeface.FromFile or FromData)
Implement manual font fallback: check if the typeface contains the glyph with SKFont.ContainsGlyphs, and use a different typeface for characters that fail
nextQuestions: Does upstream Skia have a fix for iOS 18 PingFang font format changes?
Is the issue in the cmap table parsing or in how CoreText exposes the new font format?
Does the issue affect macOS Sequoia (which also ships new PingFang) or only iOS 18?
Does using HarfBuzz shaper fully resolve the issue or only partially?

resolution.hypothesis: Apple changed the PingFang SC font to a new undocumented format in iOS 18. Skia's CoreText font port reads font tables (cmap, glyf, loca) via CTFontCopyTable/CGFontCopyTableForTag, and the new format causes incorrect glyph mapping for the degree symbol (U+00B0). System renderers (UILabel, CoreText) use higher-level APIs with automatic font fallback, so they still render correctly.

proposals.title: Bundle a known-good font
proposals.description: Instead of relying on the system PingFang SC font, bundle a known-good Chinese font (e.g., Noto Sans SC) with the app and load it with SKTypeface.FromFile or SKTypeface.FromData. This avoids the iOS 18 PingFang format issue entirely.

proposals.title: Use HarfBuzz text shaping
proposals.description: Use SkiaSharp.HarfBuzz and SKShaper to perform text shaping, which uses HarfBuzz's own font parsing and may handle the new PingFang format correctly. This is also recommended for any complex text rendering.

proposals.title: Update Skia to version with iOS 18 font fix
proposals.description: Investigate whether upstream Skia (Chromium) has landed a fix for iOS 18 PingFang font format changes. If so, update the Skia submodule to incorporate the fix. This is the proper long-term fix.

resolution.recommendedReason: Highest confidence workaround that completely avoids the platform-specific font format issue. Bundling Noto Sans SC is straightforward and gives consistent rendering across all iOS versions.

actionability.reason: Real rendering bug with complete repro, caused by iOS 18 font format change. Needs investigation into whether upstream Skia has a fix and whether HarfBuzz shaping resolves it.

actions.description: Confirm existing labels and add native area label
Acknowledge the iOS 18 font format change as root cause, suggest bundling a font as a workaround
actions.comment:
Thanks for the thorough investigation, especially tracking down the Apple Developer Forums thread about PingFang font changes in iOS 18.

This appears to be caused by Apple shipping PingFang SC in a new font format on iOS 18. Skia reads font tables via CoreText APIs, and the new format likely causes incorrect glyph mapping for the degree symbol (U+00B0). System text renderers (UILabel, CoreText) work around this with higher-level font fallback, but Skia's low-level text drawing uses a single typeface without automatic fallback.

As a workaround, you could bundle a known-good Chinese font (e.g., [Noto Sans SC](https://fonts.google.com/noto/specimen/Noto+Sans+SC)) with your app and load it via `SKTypeface.FromFile()` or `SKTypeface.FromData()`. This avoids the system font entirely and gives consistent rendering across iOS versions.

We'll investigate whether upstream Skia has addressed this font format change and whether using SkiaSharp.HarfBuzz for text shaping also resolves the issue.
